title: Correndence
author: TwiceMao
body:
Hi， @PruneTruong 
Thanks for your awesome work~
I want to confirm with you, is your correlation done on all possible pairs in the input image, or only on adjacent images?

title: python version in readme update.
author: TontonTremblay
body: Maybe update the python version to 3.10 instead of 3. Copy pasting your instruction give a non compatible python version with the requirements.txt.

title: Laggy When Running
author: TwiceMao
body: @PruneTruong  Hi，thx for your awesome work. I have 48 1200*680 color images and want to compare them with your paper. However, when running your code, the GPU is out of memory during image matching. It seems that your feature matching is all-to-all. Can you give me some advice?

title: LLFF checkpoint
author: caramel-jiao
body: Thank you for sharing your work! Excuse me for my question. I found there is no checkpoint file for 'joint_pose_nerf_training/llff' in the google drive link. May I ask if you can share LLFF pre-trained models? I'm realy looking forward to it. Additionally, compared to other sparf train_setting files, there are no settings about 'corres_weight' and 'settings_model.nerf' in 'train_settings/joint_pose_nerf_training/llff/sparf.py'. Should I modify the LLFF train_setting file to match DTU or REPLICA? Thank you for your time!

title: There is no definition and implementation of the function "render_by_slices_w_custom_rays"
author: yuanjie0217
body: This function appears in the source/models/render.py file, but I did not find the implementation code of this function

title: NaN in Rays
author: FelTris
body:
Hi, thanks for the cool work! 
I think I know where the NaNs in the ray computation come from. For the LLFF dataloader if the bounds.min() is zero the scale factor will be divided by zero and therefore also the translational component of the poses will be scaled with this NaN value. 
At least that was the case when I tried it on some custom data. 
Hope this helps some people!

title: Questions about batch setting and out of memory.
author: yfqi
body:
I have a question: When I train my own dataset with joint_pose_nerf_training and train_sub = 6, it works fine. However when I train the network with nerf_training_w_gt_poses and train_sun=6, it shows out of memory. 
So:
1. I wonder what differences about dataloaders between them two and what I can do to solve it. 
2. I also wonder how I can set the batches in your networks. Thank you

title: The difference between PSNR, PSNR_f, PSNR_c
author: YJ-142150
body:
Thanks for your great work.
But I have a question about three PSNR values during training and testing.

During training, there were PSNR, PSNR_fine. 
What are difference between these two?
It seems the two have different values.

Also, there were only PSNR_f, PSNR_c after testing.
What are difference between these two?
It also seems the two have different values.
In this case, how can I get PSNR value just like the metrics you posted?

title: Hardware spec
author: SoninVision
body:
First of all, thank you for your great work!

I've been studying NeRF recently and planning to set up my workstation to research NeRF.

So if possible, I would like you to share the information about your workstation's hardware spec that you used during this study. (CPU, RAM capacity, etc.) I found through the paper that you used a single Nvidia A100 to train the model. 

It will be a great help to me.

Thank you again!

title: Question regarding the LLFF data preprocessing
author: xiexh20
body:
Hi, 

Thanks a lot for the amazing work and releasing the code. 

I am a bit confused about the data preprocessing steps you applied to the LLFF data. 
1. If I understand it correctly, in the code snippet below, the camera pose is swapped to change from [down right back] to [right up back]. [reference in NerF repo](https://github.com/bmild/nerf/issues/34#issuecomment-616175083). However, why is the last axis being swapped instead of the second axis? as shown in the [original code ](https://github.com/bmild/nerf/blob/master/load_llff.py#L250)
https://github.com/google-research/sparf/blob/3fd450eae888724d88da89f0b4bf220529a48ad6/source/datasets/llff.py#L95-L98

2. Why a pose flip is applied to the raw camera to world pose? 
https://github.com/google-research/sparf/blob/3fd450eae888724d88da89f0b4bf220529a48ad6/source/datasets/llff.py#L182-L187

Thank you so much for your time and explaination!

title: assert norm_scale.mean() == 300 in the DTU
author: zhirui-gao
body: I use dtu dataset, but  norm_scale equal to [324.6,324.6,324.6].
